title: Weekly Vessel Valuations Report, December 09 2025
summary:
Main
in Weekly Vessel Valuations Report 09/12/2025
Image reference: 2025-12-09_weekly-vessel-valuations-report-december-09-2025_img1.png
Bulkers: There was less movement across the sector than we observed last week. Older Handysize vesselscontinued to firm this week amid more strong pricing. Mid age Capes also firmed somewhat with the conclusion of the Pacifist sale
Capesize Pacifist (181,500 DWT, 2011, Koyo Dock) sold SS/DD Due for USD 32 mil, VV Value USD 31.9 mil
Ultramax CMB Jordaens (63,400 DWT, 2019, Tadotsu Imabari) sold inc TC (USD 13,000 pd) for USD 30 mil, VV Value USD 30.1 mil
Handysize Bao Shun (33,700 DWT, 2005, Oshima) sold to a Middle Eastern buyer for USD 8.2 mil, VV Value USD 8.3 mil
Tankers: VV VLCC Tanker values have firmed this week as they track the recent surge in earnings
VLCC Towada (305,800 DWT, Jul 2006, Mitsubishi HI) sold by NYK Line for USD 45.7 mil, VV Value USD 44.45 mil
Suezmax Sapphira (150,200 DWT, Feb 2008, Universal) sold DD Due by Bocimar for USD 37 mil, VV Value USD 36.24 mil
MR2 (Chemical/Product) LVM Warrior (50,500 DWT, Jan 2015, Hyundai Mipo) sold by Les Vents Marine for USD 33.3 mil, VV Value USD 31.76 mil
MR2 (Chemical/Product) UACC Manam
